results.title: Turn Figma Designs Into Native Mobile Apps - Bilt
results.content:
Become a Bilt Partner.Read More

NewFigma import just landed in Bilt

# Turn your Figma designs into a real app

Drop your Figma frames into Bilt and watch them become a real app for iPhone and Android, looking just like you designed it. No handoff, no rebuilding by hand.

Start BuildingGet the Figma plugin

A screen running as a native app built by Bilt

how it works

## From Figma to app in three steps

No handoff docs, no redlines, no rebuilding screens by hand. You bring the design, Bilt builds the app.

01plugin

### Add your Figma file

Open your design in Figma, run the Bilt plugin, and hit “Add this file to Bilt.” Your frames show up in seconds, thumbnails and all.

02pick

### Pick the frames you want [...] ## Made for designers and the people they build with

Bring your designs into every build, and own everything that comes out.

### Built from your real design

Bilt builds from what's actually in your Figma file, your layout and your intent, not a blurry guess from a screenshot.

### No Figma account needed

Once a file's added to the project, anyone can pull its frames into a chat. Designers design, builders build.

### A real app, iPhone and Android

Your designs become a real app, smooth and fast, that you can open on your phone right away.

### You own the code

Bilt puts your app's code on GitHub. Change it, build on it, or take it and go, whenever you want. It's yours.

Turn your first frame into an app

Free to start. Your design could be running on your phone today. [...] 02pick

### Pick the frames you want

Choose the exact screens or pieces you want to build. Anyone on the project can grab them, no Figma account needed.

03build

### Bilt builds the real thing

Bilt turns your design into a real app you can open on your phone and send straight to the App Store.

true to your design

## What you design is what ships

Your spacing, your fonts, your colors, all carried over exactly as you set them. No guesswork, no "close enough." Just your design, running for real.

 Your spacing, fonts, and colors, exactly
 Built from your real design, never a screenshot
 Change anything after just by asking

what you get

results.title: I tried Figma Make — here’s what it gets right (and what’s still broken) - LogRocket Blog
results.content:
## Over 200k developers and product managers use LogRocket to create better digital experiences

Learn more →

The unusual aspect is that all the correct details are already present in the Figma file. The problem arises when developers manually write the code based on the design. This step is where many errors start.

Figma Make could fix this. When a designer finishes the work, the AI will review the screens and request any missing details. These details include colors not linked to variables or text not using styles. After that, Figma Make will turn the design into real code. [...] But if Figma fine-tunes an AI model for this purpose, it could better understand how to create flows. Many products share common solutions, so the AI model could fill in the details without needing every piece of information from the user:

## AI shouldn’t make UX decisions alone

One of the most frustrating aspects of Figma Make is that it often makes unintended decisions when I request a simple change or flow.

For example, I once asked Figma Make to fix a radio button that wasn’t working. Instead of fixing it, it changed it to a select menu. The select component worked, but I still wanted the radio button.

To fix this, Figma Make should request user feedback before completing a task. [...] For example, I built a small product for myself using Cursor (another AI tool). It helped me create Airbnb-style icons. The idea was the same — build a tool that helps me work faster.

One example could be a tool that generates error messages for a product, a tool that creates design tokens, or a tool that overlays a magnifying glass over an image.

Instead of looking for a basic tool to meet a specific need, we can build it with Figma Make. It’s also possible that we’ll be able to build Figma plugins this way, not just apps inside Figma Make. That could be a big benefit for any UX team that needs a custom tool and wants to create it themselves:

## But beware: Sameness is the real threat

results.title: How to Design and Build a Real Working Mobile App in 48 hrs ...
results.content:
## Final Result

I did run into a few snags trying to publish after I made some manual adjustments but after some troubleshooting and guidance from Buzzy’s support team, I successfully published a fully functional app. The app allowed users to create and manage budget categories in real-time, directly within Figma. I realized that when publishing issues arise, clearing the workspace and republishing from Figma is a good fix.

🗃️

Want the Figma Project Files? Download them here →

results.title: Changelog | Bilt
results.content:
TL;DR

 🚀 Launched Bilt MCP client — connect it to OpenClaw (or other agents) and build mobile.
 🎨 Major UI overhaul — new landing page + improved agent interface.
 🛠 Free error fixes — if the agent makes a mistake, one-click fix at zero token cost.
 ⚡ Parallel sub-agents — faster generation via multi-agent execution.
 🌍 Language matching — agent replies in the same language you prompt it in.
 🐛 Multiple bug fixes + token rewards for reporting new ones.

We released our Bilt MCP client. Now Your claw-y friends can now build mobile apps without the hassle of setting up the development environment and Apple’s guidelines. Just add our MCP to Your OpenClaw or other agent and start building!

More information: [...] Become a Bilt Ambassador.Read More

# Changelog

New updates, improvements, and fixes to Bilt.

May 19, 2026

## Payments

Payments

Ever seen a happy mobile developer that had integrated payments into their app? I hadn’t until now.

Up to this point, it has been one of the most annoying parts of building a mobile app. First you go and set them up in ASC. After that you pick your poison, SuperWall or RevenueCat. They force you to learn a completely new system that lacks any user friendliness. They feel like another SaaS that you must learn.  
  
We have seen countless people fail their apps at that point, that’s why we made it dead simple. Single prompt, that’s all it takes.

It used to take months to implement your in-app purchases into your app. With Bilt, it now takes a single prompt. [...] March 25, 2026

## Webtoapp Feature

Webtoapp Feature

You built your web app, either with Lovable or Replit. Now take it further.

With one click, bilt.me converts your existing web project into a fully native React Native mobile app. Same product, now on the iOS App Store.

No rebuilding from scratch. No hiring a mobile developer. Just your app, on the App Store.

Simply sync your GitHub repository through the project settings and the agent can get started!

If you need help to get started, book a quick onboarding call with us and we'll help you get your web app converted.

👉 Book your onboarding call

March 23, 2026
